response:
*KEEP THE AREA CLEAN*  
BRUSH GENTLY AND THOROUGHLY AT LEAST TWICE A DAY WITH A SOFT-BRISTLED TOOTHBRUSH.  
RINSE YOUR MOUTH WITH WATER AFTER EVERY MEAL.  
FLOSS CAREFULLY TO REMOVE FOOD PARTICLES.  

*AVOID CERTAIN FOODS*  
STAY AWAY FROM HARD, STICKY, OR CHEWWY FOODS.  
AVOID EXTREME HOT OR COLD FOODS AND BEVERAGES.  
USE TEMPORARY FILLING MATERIALS AS INSTRUCTIONS SAYS.  

*MONITOR FOR CHANGES*  
KEEP AN EYE ON THE TOOTH FOR ANY CHANGES OR SIGNS OF INFECTION.  
REMEMBER TO SEEK DENTAL CARE AS SOON AS POSSIBLE.  
DENTAL EMERGENCIES REQUIRE PROMPT ATTENTION.  

*USE SALINE RINSE*  
RINSE YOUR MOUTH WITH A SALINE SOLUTION.  
MIX 1/2 TEASPOON OF SALT IN 8 OUNCES OF WARM WATER.  
RINSE GENTLY TO REDUCE INFLAMMATION AND BACTERIA.  

*CHOOSE THE RIGHT FOOD*  
CHOOSE FOOD THAT IS NOT HARD OR STICKY.  
CHOOSE FOOD THAT IS NOT EXTREMELY HOT OR COLD.  
CHOOSE FOOD THAT IS NOT CHEWY OR STICKY.  

*BE CAREFUL WITH PRESSURE*  
CHEW ON THE OPPOSITE SIDE OF YOUR MOUTH.  
AVOID PUTTING PRESSURE ON THE DAMAGED TOOTH.  
KEEP AN EYE ON THE TOOTH FOR ANY CHANGES OR SIGNS OF INFECTION.  

*VISIT DENTAL CARE*  
VISIT DENTAL CARE OPTIONS IN YOUR CURRENT LOCATION.  
DENTAL SCHOOLS, EXPATRIATE COMMUNITIES, OR LOCAL HEALTHCARE SERVICES CAN OFFER GUIDANCE.  
DO NOT WAIT FOR THE FULL DURATION OF YOUR STAY ABROAD.
